relProperties.description: Second line changes from Yang to Yin - Dragon appears in the field. It furthers one to see the great man. The creative begins to emerge.
Fifth line changes from Yang to Yin - Flying dragon in the heavens. It furthers one to see the great man. The creative force at its peak.
Sixth line changes from Yang to Yin - Arrogant dragon will have cause to repent. Even creativity must know its limits.
First line changes from Yang to Yin - The dragon lies hidden. Do not act. The creative force must wait for the proper time to manifest.
Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - Wavering flight over the depths. No blame. Testing the creative potential.
Third line changes from Yang to Yin - All day long the superior man is creatively active. At nightfall his mind is still beset with cares. Danger. No blame.
Second line changes from Yin to Yang - Bearing with the uncultured in gentleness, fording the river with resolution.
Third line changes from Yin to Yang - No plain not followed by a slope. No going not followed by a return.
Sixth line changes from Yang to Yin - The wall falls back into the moat. Use no army now. Make your commands known within your own town.
Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - He flutters down, not boasting of his wealth, together with his neighbor.
First line changes from Yin to Yang - When ribbon grass is pulled up, the sod comes with it. Each according to his kind.
Fifth line changes from Yang to Yin - The sovereign I gives his daughter in marriage. This brings blessing and supreme good fortune.
Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - Perseverance brings good fortune. Remorse disappears. The hedge opens; there is no entanglement.
Fifth line changes from Yang to Yin - Loses the goat with ease. No remorse.
Sixth line changes from Yang to Yin - A goat butts against a hedge. It cannot go backward, it cannot go forward. Nothing serves to further.
Third line changes from Yang to Yin - The inferior man works through power. The superior man does not act thus. To continue is dangerous.
First line changes from Yang to Yin - Power in the toes. Continuing brings misfortune. This is certain.
Second line changes from Yang to Yin - Perseverance brings good fortune.
Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - If you are sincere, blood vanishes and fear gives way. No blame.
Second line changes from Yang to Yin - He allows himself to be drawn into returning. Good fortune.
Third line changes from Yin to Yang - The spokes burst out of the wagon wheels. Man and wife roll their eyes.
Fifth line changes from Yang to Yin - If you are sincere and loyally attached, you are rich in your neighbor.
Sixth line changes from Yang to Yin - The rain comes, there is rest. Virtue accumulates. Perseverance brings good fortune to women.
First line changes from Yang to Yin - Return to the way. How could there be blame in this? Good fortune.
Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - Waiting in blood. Get out of the pit. Escaping dangerous delay.
Second line changes from Yang to Yin - Waiting on sand. There is some gossip. The end brings good fortune.
Sixth line changes from Yang to Yin - One falls into the pit. Three uninvited guests arrive. Honor them for eventual fortune.
Fifth line changes from Yang to Yin - Waiting at meat and drink. Perseverance brings good fortune. Comfortable patience.
First line changes from Yin to Yang - Waiting in the meadow. It furthers one to abide in what endures. Patient preparation.
Third line changes from Yin to Yang - Waiting in mud brings about the arrival of the enemy. Careless waiting invites trouble.
Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - He makes a difference between himself and his neighbor. No blame.
Sixth line changes from Yang to Yin - He is blessed by heaven. Good fortune. Nothing that does not further.
Second line changes from Yin to Yang - A big wagon for loading. One may undertake something. No blame.
First line changes from Yang to Yin - No relationship with what is harmful. There is no blame in this.
Fifth line changes from Yang to Yin - He whose truth is accessible yet dignified has good fortune.
Third line changes from Yin to Yang - A prince offers it to the Son of Heaven. A petty man cannot do this.
Third line changes from Yin to Yang - A good horse that follows others. Awareness of danger, with perseverance, furthers.
First line changes from Yang to Yin - Danger is at hand. It furthers one to desist.
Sixth line changes from Yang to Yin - One attains the way of heaven. Success.
Second line changes from Yin to Yang - The axletrees are taken from the wagon.
Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - The headboard of a young bull. Great good fortune.
Fifth line changes from Yang to Yin - The tusk of a gelded boar. Good fortune.
First line changes from Yang to Yin - Mighty in the forward-striding toes. When one goes and is not equal to the task, one makes a mistake.
Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - There is no skin on his thighs, and walking comes hard. If a man were to let himself be led like a sheep, remorse would disappear.
Fifth line changes from Yang to Yin - In dealing with weeds, firm resolution is necessary. Walking in the middle remains free of blame.
Sixth line changes from Yang to Yin - No cry. In the end misfortune comes.
Third line changes from Yang to Yin - To be powerful in the cheekbones brings misfortune. The superior man is firmly resolved.
Second line changes from Yang to Yin - A cry of alarm. Arms at evening and at night. Fear nothing.

relProperties.description: Fourth line changes from Yin to Yang - He who acts at the command of the highest remains without blame.

Fourth line changes from Yin to Yang - A tied-up sack. No blame, no praise. Discrete containment.

Fifth line changes from Yin to Yang - A yellow lower garment brings supreme good fortune. Modest receptivity elevated.

Third line changes from Yin to Yang - Enthusiasm that looks upward creates remorse. Hesitation brings remorse.

Fifth line changes from Yin to Yang - Persistently ill, and still does not die.
Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - The source of enthusiasm. He achieves great things.
First line changes from Yin to Yang - Enthusiasm that expresses itself brings misfortune.

Sixth line changes from Yin to Yang - Contemplation of his life. The superior man is without blame.
Second line changes from Yang to Yin - Contemplation through the crack of the door. Furthering for the perseverance of a woman.
Third line changes from Yin to Yang - Contemplation of my life decides the choice between advance and retreat.
Fifth line changes from Yin to Yang - Contemplation of my life. The superior man is without blame.
Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - Contemplation of the light of the kingdom. It furthers one to exert influence as the guest of a king.

Fourth line changes from Yin to Yang - Hold to him outwardly also. Perseverance brings good fortune. Open alliance.
First line changes from Yin to Yang - Hold to him in truth and loyalty; this is without blame. Sincere alliance.
Sixth line changes from Yang to Yin - He finds no head for holding together. Misfortune. Leaderless cooperation fails.
Second line changes from Yang to Yin - Hold to him inwardly. Perseverance brings good fortune. Inner solidarity.
Third line changes from Yang to Yin - All are in accord. Remorse disappears.
Fifth line changes from Yin to Yang - Remorse disappears. Take not gain and loss to heart. Undertakings bring good fortune.

Second line changes from Yin to Yang - Progressing, but in sorrow. Perseverance brings good fortune.

Fourth line changes from Yin to Yang - Progress like a hamster. Perseverance brings danger.
Fourth line changes from Yin to Yang - The bed is split up to the skin. Misfortune.
Third line changes from Yin to Yang - He splits with them. No blame.

Fifth line changes from Yin to Yang - A shoal of fishes. Favor comes through the court ladies. Everything furthers.
Sixth line changes from Yin to Yang - There is a large fruit still uneaten. The superior man receives a carriage.
First line changes from Yang to Yin - The leg of the bed is split. Those who persevere are destroyed. Misfortune.
Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - Great good fortune. No blame.
First line changes from Yin to Yang - If you are sincere, but not to the end, there will sometimes be confusion, sometimes gathering together.
Third line changes from Yin to Yang - Gathering together amid sighs. Nothing that would further. Going is without blame. Slight humiliation.
Sixth line changes from Yang to Yin - Lamenting and sighing, floods of tears. No blame.
Second line changes from Yin to Yang - Letting oneself be drawn brings good fortune and remains without blame. If one is sincere, it furthers one to bring even a small offering.

Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - He who can be persevering remains without blame.
Sixth line changes from Yin to Yang - Innocent action brings misfortune. Nothing furthers.
Second line changes from Yin to Yang - Quiet return. Good fortune.
Sixth line changes from Yin to Yang - Missing the return. Misfortune.
Third line changes from Yin to Yang - Repeated return. Danger. No blame.
Fourth line changes from Yin to Yang - Walking in the midst of others, one returns alone.
First line changes from Yang to Yin - Return from a short distance. No need for remorse. Great good fortune.
Fifth line changes from Yin to Yang - Noblehearted return. No remorse.

Fifth line changes from Yang to Yin - If in truth you have a kind heart, ask not. Supreme good fortune. Truly, kindness will be recognized as your virtue.
Sixth line changes from Yang to Yin - He brings increase to no one. Indeed, someone even strikes him. He does not keep his heart constantly steady. Misfortune.
First line changes from Yang to Yin - It furthers one to accomplish great deeds. Supreme good fortune. No blame.
Fourth line changes from Yin to Yang - Horse and wagon part. Strive for union. Overcoming separation through effort.
Fifth line changes from Yin to Yang - Difficulties in blessing. Small perseverance brings good fortune. Moderation in struggle.
First line changes from Yin to Yang - Hesitation and hindrance. It furthers one to remain persevering. Seek helpers in difficulty.
Sixth line changes from Yang to Yin - Horse and wagon part. Bloody tears flow. When effort leads to suffering.

Third line changes from Yin to Yang - Turning away from nourishment. Perseverance brings misfortune. Do not act thus for ten years. Nothing serves to further.
First line changes from Yang to Yin - You let your magic tortoise go, and look at me with the corners of your mouth drooping. Misfortune.
Second line changes from Yin to Yang - Turning to the summit for nourishment, deviating from the path to seek nourishment from the hill. Continuing to do this brings misfortune.
Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - Turning to the summit for provision of nourishment brings good fortune. Spying about with sharp eyes like a tiger with insatiable craving. No blame.
Fifth line changes from Yin to Yang - Turning away from the path. To remain persevering brings good fortune. One should not cross the great water.
Fourth line changes from Yang to Yin - Following creates success. Perseverance brings misfortune.
Third line changes from Yin to Yang - If one clings to the strong man, one loses the little boy.
First line changes from Yang to Yin - The standard is changing. Perseverance brings good fortune.
Fifth line changes from Yin to Yang - Sincere in the good. Good fortune.
Sixth line changes from Yin to Yang - He meets with strong commitment. He is still further bound and follows.

North compass anchor provides transcendent polar foundation for entire wheel structure
South compass anchor provides manifestation polar foundation complementing north transcendence
East compass anchor provides emergence gateway initiating clockwise wheel circulation
West compass anchor provides integration gateway completing clockwise circulation cycle
Central Unity serves dual role as compass convergence point and origin source for all 360 degree positions
North embodies Kun's pure receptive principle in the Earlier Heaven arrangement, providing the foundational yin matrix from which all manifestation emerges
North expresses Kan's dangerous depths in the Later Heaven arrangement, where winter's apparent stillness conceals the yang core of spiritual sincerity
North-South axis creates the primary vertical polarity between transcendent reception and manifested expression
North to East represents the quarterly progression from transcendent depth to emerging initiative
South embodies Qian's pure creative principle in the Earlier Heaven arrangement, providing the foundational yang impulse that initiates all manifestation
South expresses Li's clinging fire in the Later Heaven arrangement, where summer's maximum yang manifests as consciousness that depends on objects for illumination
South to West represents the quarterly progression from full expression to reflective integration
East embodies Li's clinging fire in the Earlier Heaven arrangement, representing the primordial light of consciousness that illuminates the eternal dawn
East expresses Zhen's thunder arousal in the Later Heaven arrangement, where spring's emerging yang breaks through winter's stillness with shocking new life
East-West axis creates the primary horizontal polarity between emerging initiative and integrating reflection
East to South represents the quarterly progression from emerging arousal to full manifestation
West embodies Kan's abysmal water in the Earlier Heaven arrangement, representing the primordial depths of unconscious wisdom that flow beneath surface awareness
West expresses Dui's joyous lake in the Later Heaven arrangement, where autumn's gathering yin creates the reflective surface through which accumulated wisdom is joyously communicated
West to North represents the quarterly progression from integrative reflection back to transcendent depth
Central Unity serves as the quintessential convergence point where all four compass directions meet in perfect balance
